statement: Decifer's single objective is to build alpha — a statistical edge durable enough to remain profitable after costs, slippage, and regime changes. The current phase is proving that edge on a paper account: every paper trade is a data point used to validate the 9-dimension signal engine and 6-agent pipeline. Live trading, cloud hosting, and any multi-user future are conditional consequences that only become real if paper-trading alpha is proven first. If the edge is not real, nothing downstream matters.
end_state_capabilities: Measurable, persistent alpha — Information Coefficient tracked per signal dimension and per regime
Reliable bot execution — no silent failures, state drift, broker desyncs, or hot-path regressions
Sufficient closed-trade volume across regimes to statistically validate the edge (Phase C gate: ≥200 closed trades)
Walk-forward validated signal weights — not static, not overfit, honest about regime dependence
Regime-aware performance reporting — separate PnL and IC by BULL_TRENDING / BEAR_TRENDING / CHOPPY / PANIC
Live trading on a single real-money account, only after paper alpha is proven
Cloud hosting and multi-user platform — conditional on live-account alpha holding up
success_definition: Paper alpha is proven when IC is positive and stable across regimes over ≥200 closed trades, signal weights hold up under walk-forward testing, and the bot runs autonomously for extended periods without execution regressions. Only then does the live-trading question become worth asking. Everything beyond live trading is conditional on that question being answered affirmatively with real money.
stage_gate_note: Stages beyond paper_trading_single_account are not on a roadmap — they are gated. Each transition requires statistical proof from the prior stage, not a calendar date. The bot does not advance because time passes; it advances because the edge is demonstrated.
